notes: Models vibe's presession proc-<PID> → real session_<ts> dir → /compact → session_<ts2> dir lifecycle. Compaction takes the SAME reset_session dir-rotation code path as /clear (agent_loop.compact → _reset_session, keep_parent=True), so it surfaces as old-session-ends + new-session-born, NOT a single continuous row; the append-only messages.jsonl is never truncated so there is no append-tailer bug. compact_boundary requires a fresh session_<ts2> dir (new_session:true) minted by /compact — born WORKING because its first persisted line is the injected (role=user,injected=True) compaction-context message (parser → user_message, opens a turn), not yet settled. old_session_ended requires transcript_removed for the v1 session (same-PID cleanup, pid_manager.go:566-576). The post-compaction turn drives working→ready on the new dir, proving continuation. BACKFLOW (live basic-turn recording): mistral-vibe is a PRESESSION adapter — a transient proc-<PID> row is born ready BEFORE the real session_<ts> transcript dir reconciles under a DIFFERENT session_id; modeled as presession_birth → session_birth(new_session) → pid_bind (birth templated from the codex sibling), and /compact then mints a SECOND new_session (compact_boundary). Offline replay cannot reproduce vibe's meta.json sidecar, so model + tokens are UNVERIFIABLE offline — observations assert agent only (cost_nonzero=false, tokens_nonzero=false), matching kiro-cli. Emission ORDERING and the born-working state are provisional — record tightens the max_delay bounds.

text: proc-<PID> pre-session appears in ready within 1s of launch (PID bound before the transcript dir exists)
Real session_<ts> dir reconciles (different session_id) after the first prompt lands, born ready — proc-<PID> hands off to the real vibe session
vibe PID bound to the real session (DiscoverPID by cwd + cmdline)
First user prompt populates context: ready → working on the session_<ts> session
First turn completes back to ready (same session dir)

/compact rotates to a fresh session_<ts2> dir never seen before (new_session:true) — the compaction boundary, minted after the first turn. Born WORKING: its first persisted line is the injected compaction-context user message (no turn_done yet). expected_state is provisional (born-working vs a brief ready) — record settles it from the live /compact.
Old (pre-compaction) session_<ts> dir disappears — same-PID cleanup emits transcript_removed when the new session binds the same vibe PID (bound provisional pending record).
Post-compaction user prompt drives the NEW session working (must NOT match a stale transition on the old dir) — proves the session continues after compaction
Post-compaction turn completes back to ready on the new session; final state ready
